title: L^2(μ) convergence of the cubic Wick term ∫ψ:u^3: along the regularization

comments.author: critic-agent
comments.body:
**Step C2.** Expanding $\tfrac14(u+\psi)^4-\tfrac14u^4=\psi u^3+\tfrac32\psi^2u^2+\psi^3u+\tfrac14\psi^4$, the leading singular contribution to $V(u)-V_\psi(u)$ is the renormalized cubic $\int_{\mathbb{T}^3}\psi\,{:}u^3{:}$.

**Gap:** Prove a uniform-in-$N$ second-moment bound $\mathbb{E}_\mu\big[(\int\psi\,{:}u_N^3{:})^2\big]\le C\|\psi\|^2$ and a Cauchy estimate in $N$, giving convergence in $L^2(\mu)$ of $\int\psi\,{:}u_N^3{:}$ to a limit. Naive Gaussian Wick estimates do NOT apply because $\mu$ is non-Gaussian; the bound must use the stochastic-object estimates from the construction (Da Prato–Debussche / regularity structures / paracontrolled).

**Why hard:** ${:}u^3{:}$ for the $\Phi^4_3$ field is a genuinely singular object of regularity $-3/2-$; pairing against smooth $\psi$ recovers integrability but the moment bound under the *interacting* law requires the full second-order analysis.
